VAL-W8A8-001.evidence: test_w8a8_gemv.py: ALL TESTS PASSED. 4096x4096: int32_rel=0.0005 fp16_rel=0.2% max_abs=0.00767 PASS. 11008x4096: int32_rel=0.0005 fp16_rel=0.2% max_abs=0.00775 PASS. Small shapes (64x128, 256x512, 4096x128) all PASS. INT32 accumulation test: max_rel=0.00162 PASS. Scale correctness verified. v_dot4_i32_i8 intrinsic (__builtin_amdgcn_sdot4) verified present in source. Well within 5% relative error threshold.

nonBlockingIssues.description: The _launch_gemv_w8a8 engine method requires a D2H transfer of 4 bytes (scale_a scalar) after activation quantization, before the W8A8 GEMV kernel call. This is noted in the engine code comments. For decode at high throughput, this 4-byte D2H + host copy + value injection into ctypes adds small but non-zero latency.
nonBlockingIssues.suggestedFix: Modify gemv_w8a8 and gemv_w4a8 kernel signatures to accept scale_a as a GPU pointer (const float*) instead of by-value float. This avoids the D2H sync entirely. Medium priority optimization.

nonBlockingIssues.description: W8A8 GEMV bandwidth efficiency is 27-46% of peak HBM (229-392 GB/s vs 860 GB/s theoretical). This is lower than the W4A16 v3_t16 kernel which achieves ~53-62% of peak. The W8A8 kernel reads 2x the weight bytes (INT8 vs INT4) so being slower than W4A16 is expected for memory-bandwidth-bound decode.
nonBlockingIssues.suggestedFix: Use W8A8 only for prefill (M>1) where it can become compute-bound. For decode (M=1), W4A16 remains the preferred format. This tradeoff is correctly documented in bench_w8a8.py.

sharedStateObservations.observation: W8A8 GEMV performance characteristics: 4096x4096 → 78us 213GB/s; 11008x4096 → 139us 325GB/s. W4A16 v3_t16 is 2.4x faster for decode (M=1) because W8A8 reads 2x weight data. W8A8 competitive for prefill. __builtin_amdgcn_sdot4 maps to v_dot4_i32_i8.
sharedStateObservations.suggestedContent: Document in architecture.md: W8A8 GEMV decode performance is bandwidth-limited (2x weight bytes vs W4A16). Recommended: use W8A8 for prefill (GEMM path) and W4A16 for decode (GEMV path). v_dot4_i32_i8 via __builtin_amdgcn_sdot4 confirmed working on gfx906.

summary: Feature fully satisfies VAL-W8A8-001. W8A8 GEMV uses __builtin_amdgcn_sdot4 (v_dot4_i32_i8), INT32 accumulation, FP32 scale epilogue → FP16 output. All correctness tests pass with fp16_rel < 0.3% (well within 5% threshold). Performance correctly characterized as memory-bandwidth-bound for decode. Scale computation correct. No blocking issues.
